rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Simonson (2003) for a specific theoretical claim: when a no-buy option is available, compromise choice weakens because deferral is a less effortful response. That claim is used in hypothesis development about choice deferral and compromise under reduced cognitive resources, and it also guides study design decisions about including no-buy options and treating forced-choice settings as a robustness condition. This is more than a passing mention, because the cited paper helps structure both framing and experimental logic.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as key prior evidence on how forced versus free choice changes deferral and compromise behavior. It builds its conceptual setup and major design choices around that established forced-choice/no-buy mechanism.
